findings.description: Multiple potholes visible in deteriorated asphalt road surface. The most prominent is in the foreground/center of image 4 (South view) showing a depression with missing asphalt, exposed darker base material, and irregular broken edges. Additional pavement deterioration with patching and cracking visible throughout the area. The road surface shows significant wear with multiple areas of damage.

candidates.description: Image 1 shows a yellow taxi and dark car parked along a corrugated metal fence, with what appears to be standing water or mud on the unpaved/deteriorated road surface in the foreground. The brown area could be muddy water pooling, but no clear pothole edges, exposed base material, or distinct depression boundaries are visible - just murky water covering the surface.

The road surface shows aged concrete/asphalt pavement with visible wear, cracking patterns, and old patch repairs. The pavement has uneven texture and some deterioration but no discrete holes where asphalt is physically missing. The street has parked vehicles along both sides with moderate surface degradation typical of older urban streets.

The road surface shows minor surface cracks visible in the concrete pavement, particularly visible in the downward-facing image. The cracks appear as linear features running across the pavement but there is no missing asphalt material, no exposed base layer, and no depression or hole. The pavement is concrete rather than asphalt, showing typical joint lines and minor surface weathering.

The road surface shows general wear with visible surface cracks in the concrete/asphalt pavement. No holes or depressions where material is missing are visible. The pavement shows typical aging patterns with fine cracking but maintains structural integrity.

The road surface shows some longitudinal cracking visible in the asphalt near the curb junction in Image 1, with a visible crack line running along the pavement edge. The street appears to be in a residential/commercial area with well-maintained sidewalks featuring decorative tile paving. No actual potholes or missing asphalt material observed.

The road surface shows extensive cracking patterns throughout, particularly visible in images 3 and 4, with interconnected surface cracks spreading across the pavement. No actual holes or depressions with missing asphalt are visible - the damage is limited to surface-level cracking without exposed base material or depth.

The road surface shows weathered asphalt with visible surface cracking patterns throughout the pavement. No holes or depressions with missing asphalt are visible - the dark areas are shadows and surface texture variation, not actual potholes. The cracks are primarily surface-level fatigue cracking without exposed base material.

The road surface shows extensive patch repairs with darker asphalt patches visible throughout the pavement. The surface has significant wear with visible seams and color variations from multiple repair patches. No discrete potholes with missing asphalt are visible - the darker irregular areas are prior repairs, not holes.

Multiple potholes visible in deteriorated asphalt road surface. The most prominent is in the foreground/center of image 4 (South view) showing a depression with missing asphalt, exposed darker base material, and irregular broken edges. Additional pavement deterioration with patching and cracking visible throughout the area. The road surface shows significant wear with multiple areas of damage.

debug_points.description: The road surface shows general wear with some cracking and uneven pavement joints visible in the concrete/asphalt surface. The intersection area shows typical aging with surface deterioration but no discrete holes where asphalt is missing. Some minor cracking patterns are visible but no exposed base material or depth indicating actual potholes.

The street view images show a residential urban street in Panama City with typical worn asphalt surfaces. The road shows general surface deterioration, minor cracks, and aging pavement but no discrete potholes with missing asphalt material. A yellow taxi is visible in the foreground of image 1, and various parked vehicles are seen along the street.

Image 1 shows a yellow taxi and dark car parked along a corrugated metal fence, with what appears to be standing water or mud on the unpaved/deteriorated road surface in the foreground. The brown area could be muddy water pooling, but no clear pothole edges, exposed base material, or distinct depression boundaries are visible - just murky water covering the surface.

The road surface shows aged concrete/asphalt pavement with visible wear, cracking patterns, and old patch repairs. The pavement has uneven texture and some deterioration but no discrete holes where asphalt is physically missing. The street has parked vehicles along both sides with moderate surface degradation typical of older urban streets.

The road surface shows minor surface cracks visible in the concrete pavement, particularly visible in the downward-facing image. The cracks appear as linear features running across the pavement but there is no missing asphalt material, no exposed base layer, and no depression or hole. The pavement is concrete rather than asphalt, showing typical joint lines and minor surface weathering.

The road surface shows general wear with visible surface cracks in the concrete/asphalt pavement. No holes or depressions where material is missing are visible. The pavement shows typical aging patterns with fine cracking but maintains structural integrity.

The road surface shows typical concrete/asphalt paving with visible expansion joints and minor surface cracks. The pavement has general weathering and textural variations but no discrete holes or missing material. The gray blurred area in images 1, 4, 5, and 6 is the Google Street View camera stitching artifact, not road damage.

The street views show a residential road with parked vehicles. The asphalt surface appears in fair condition with normal wear patterns but no visible potholes or significant damage. The pavement shows typical aging with some surface texture variation but no holes, depressions, or missing material.

The street view images show a residential street in Panama City with parked vehicles. The asphalt surface appears in generally good condition with normal wear patterns. There are some minor surface irregularities and discoloration typical of aged pavement, but no discrete holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material visible.

The street view shows a residential street with a parked blue SUV/crossover vehicle in the foreground. The visible asphalt surface appears intact with normal wear and texture. There is a painted curb (yellow and red) visible, along with a trash bin and small grassy area with a tree near a gated property entrance. No potholes, cracks, or significant pavement damage are visible in the accessible road surface areas.

The road surface shows some longitudinal cracking visible in the asphalt near the curb junction in Image 1, with a visible crack line running along the pavement edge. The street appears to be in a residential/commercial area with well-maintained sidewalks featuring decorative tile paving. No actual potholes or missing asphalt material observed.

The images show a well-maintained building entrance/driveway area with decorative patterned concrete pavers (gray with wave pattern) and reddish-brown pavers. There is a curved grass strip and traffic cones/bollards visible. The pavement surface shows normal concrete joint lines and minor surface wear but no potholes or significant damage.

Image 1 shows a well-maintained commercial entrance area with decorative patterned concrete/tile pavement, artificial grass median, and a Panamanian flag visible. Image 2 shows parked vehicles on an asphalt surface with corrugated metal fencing in background. The visible road surfaces appear intact without potholes or significant damage.

The street view images show a paved urban area in Panama City with decorative patterned concrete/tile pavement in the building entrance areas and standard concrete road surface. The road shows typical wear patterns with minor surface roughness and some minor cracking at joints, but no potholes or holes where asphalt is missing. The concrete road surface is intact throughout visible areas.

The road surface shows extensive cracking patterns throughout, particularly visible in images 3 and 4, with interconnected surface cracks spreading across the pavement. No actual holes or depressions with missing asphalt are visible - the damage is limited to surface-level cracking without exposed base material or depth.

The road surface shows weathered asphalt with visible surface cracking patterns throughout the pavement. No holes or depressions with missing asphalt are visible - the dark areas are shadows and surface texture variation, not actual potholes. The cracks are primarily surface-level fatigue cracking without exposed base material.

The road surface shows general wear with cracked and deteriorated concrete/asphalt. There are visible surface irregularities and minor depressions but no distinct potholes with missing asphalt or exposed base material. The pavement has aging cracks and uneven texture typical of weathered roadway.

The road surface shows extensive patch repairs with darker asphalt patches visible throughout the pavement. The surface has significant wear with visible seams and color variations from multiple repair patches. No discrete potholes with missing asphalt are visible - the darker irregular areas are prior repairs, not holes.

Multiple potholes visible in deteriorated asphalt road surface. The most prominent is in the foreground/center of image 4 (South view) showing a depression with missing asphalt, exposed darker base material, and irregular broken edges. Additional pavement deterioration with patching and cracking visible throughout the area. The road surface shows significant wear with multiple areas of damage.

The road surface shows aged asphalt with general wear and some surface irregularities. The pavement appears wet with minor surface deterioration but no discrete holes or missing asphalt material visible. The street is a narrow urban lane between buildings with parked vehicles.

The road surface shows general wear and weathering with some surface deterioration and minor cracking at joints. There is a yellow-painted curb area with a palm tree planter and a storm drain grate visible. The concrete/asphalt surface shows aging and some discoloration but no discrete holes with missing asphalt material.

The road surface shows concrete/asphalt paving in good condition with minor surface wear. Yellow directional arrows and 'NO ESTACIONE' (no parking) markings are visible. The pavement has typical expansion joints and minor surface texture variations but no holes, missing material, or significant damage.

This appears to be a private parking area or building entrance with internal driveways. The pavement shows normal wear patterns including minor surface deterioration and tire marks. Yellow painted text reading 'NO ESTACIONE' (No Parking) and 'PRIVADO' (Private) along with directional arrows are visible. No potholes or significant asphalt damage observed.
